description: Regression tests for worktree concurrency: merge lock acquisition/release, stale PID detection, lock contention, merge conflict breadcrumbs, successful merge cleanup, and auto-commit before merge.

assertions.context: Lock acquisition succeeds and returns exit code 0
Lock file is created on disk after acquisition
Lock file stores the acquiring process PID
Dead PID lock is cleaned and re-acquired
After stale cleanup, lock contains new PID
Cannot acquire lock held by live process
Contention does not corrupt existing lock
Release removes the lock file

assertions.pattern: release_lock on missing file does not error
assertions.context: Release is safe on non-existent file
Merge conflict is aborted cleanly, repo not left in merge state
Breadcrumb JSON written when merge fails
Breadcrumb records conflict as the failure reason
Worktree branch is NOT deleted on conflict
Lock is always released, even on failure
Worktree directory cleaned up after successful merge
Branch is deleted after successful merge
Prior failed-merge breadcrumb cleaned up on success
Merged content actually lands on source branch
Lock released after successful merge

assertions.pattern: branch merged and deleted \(tracked changes auto-committed\)
assertions.context: Uncommitted tracked changes are auto-committed then merged
Auto-committed tracked content lands on source branch
New untracked files are auto-committed then merged
Auto-committed untracked file content is correct
